name: Academy Bank, National Association
description_short: FDIC-insured national bank offering personal and business checking, savings, mortgages, and credit products with competitive rates on CDs and money market accounts.
description_long:
Academy Bank, National Association is a Kansas City-based bank headquartered at 1201 Walnut Street in Kansas City, Missouri. Founded and operating as a full-service financial institution, the bank holds FDIC insurance backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government, providing deposit protection up to regulatory limits. The organization has earned recognition as one of America's Most Innovative Companies by FORTUNE (2025, 2023) and was named one of Forbes' Best In-State Banks for 2025 based on customer satisfaction surveys.

Academy Bank offers a comprehensive suite of banking products including personal and business checking accounts, savings accounts, Certificates of Deposit (CDs), Premier Money Market accounts, Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs), Home Equity Lines of Credit (HELOCs), mortgages, small business loans, and credit cards. The bank advertises competitive interest rates—such as 3.75% APY on 13-month CDs and 3.65% introductory APY on Premier Money Market accounts. They provide online banking platforms, mobile app access, and loan payment services through their digital channels.

Academy Bank positions itself as a community-focused regional lender in an increasingly consolidated banking landscape. The bank commissioned research showing that 58.9% of small business owners prefer community or regional banks over national chains, aligning with their own market positioning. They emphasize personalized service through loan advisors and mortgage specialists, with customer testimonials highlighting knowledgeable staff and ease of understanding banking products. The bank also maintains awareness of fraud risks, actively warning customers about phone spoofing attempts targeting their institution.

As a traditional full-service bank, Academy Bank is best suited for customers seeking comprehensive banking relationships with local service touchpoints rather than those prioritizing only digital-first or no-fee banking. The introductory rates on savings products and HELOCs are competitive but subject to change and include standard fees and terms. The bank's strength lies in mortgage lending and small business services, making it particularly valuable for those needs, though customers should compare rates across competitors before committing. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Savings accounts with competitive interest rates
Certificates of Deposit (CDs) with rates up to 3.75% APY
Premier Money Market accounts with introductory rates
Home Equity Lines of Credit (HELOCs) with introductory APR
Mortgage lending for home purchase and refinance
Online banking platform with account access
Loan and credit card payment processing
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by U.S. Government, providing security up to regulatory limits
Competitive CD rates (3.75% APY for 13-month CDs) and money market introductory rates (3.65% APY for 6 months)
Comprehensive product suite including mortgages, business loans, HELOCs, IRAs, and credit products
Named FORTUNE America's Most Innovative Companies (2025, 2023) and Forbes Best In-State Banks 2025
Local community-focused banking with dedicated loan advisors and mortgage specialists
Online and mobile banking platform with account access and bill payment capabilities
Active fraud prevention awareness, warning customers about phone spoofing and credential threats
cons: Introductory rates on CDs and money market accounts revert to standard rates after promotional periods
Opening deposits required for savings products and CDs, creating barriers to entry for some customers
Limited information on website about checking account fees, overdraft policies, and minimum balance requirements
Standard banking hours may not accommodate all customer schedules despite online options
Rates subject to change as stated; competitive rates require rate comparison shopping
best_for: Small business owners seeking community-focused lenders with dedicated business loan advisors
Home buyers and refinancers looking for mortgage expertise and relationship-based lending
Savers seeking competitive CD and money market rates with FDIC insurance protection
Customers prioritizing local banking relationships and personalized service over purely digital alternatives
diagnosis: Academy Bank is best for customers seeking comprehensive local banking relationships with competitive rates on savings products and strong mortgage or small business lending services. The primary caveat is that advertised introductory rates are temporary; customers must understand the full terms and compare rates across competitors, as standard rates may be less competitive than promotional offerings.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
